text:
# Problem 9 — expert_token_scatter_with_weighted_forward (backward), B200

SwiGLU expert-MLP **backward** with token scatter/gather. Computes grads for
`hidden_states`, `routing_weights`, and the three expert weights `w1/w2/w3`.
Consts: `hidden_dim H = 4096`, `ffn_dim F = 14336`. Vars: `batch_seq_len B`,
`num_tokens N` (N ≤ B). All I/O **bf16**; reference upcasts to fp32 internally
(that is a *stability choice*, not a spec — see tolerances). Forward
intermediates (`w1_output, gate_output, up_output, gated_output,
expert_output`) arrive as **inputs** — nothing is recomputed from scratch.

## Op graph (semantics, not the required implementation)

```
grad_output(B,H) --gather[idx]-->      gwo(N,H)                       # gather N rows
gwo, expert_output --mul,rowsum-->      grad_sel_w(N,) --scatter--> grad_routing_weights(B,)   [OUT2]
gwo, selected_weights --row-scale-->    geo = grad_expert_output(N,H)
geo, gated_output   --GEMM G1 (K=N)-->  grad_w2_weight(H,F)                                    [OUT4]
geo, w2_weight      --GEMM G2 (K=H)-->  grad_gated(N,F)
grad_gated, up_output --mul-->          grad_gate(N,F)
grad_gated, gate_output --mul-->        grad_up(N,F)
grad_up, selected_tokens --GEMM G3(K=N)--> grad_w3_weight(F,H)                                 [OUT5]
grad_up, w3_weight  --GEMM G4 (K=F)-->  gst_w3(N,H)
grad_gate, w1_output --SiLU'-->         grad_w1o(N,F)                 # sigmoid(w1_output) recomputed
grad_w1o, selected_tokens --GEMM G5(K=N)--> grad_w1_weight(F,H)                                [OUT3]
grad_w1o, w1_weight --GEMM G6 (K=F)-->  gst_w1(N,H)
gst_w1 + gst_w3 --add, scatter[idx]-->  grad_hidden_states(B,H)       # rest zero-filled        [OUT1]
```

**6 GEMMs, each 2·N·H·F FLOPs** (all identical FLOP count). Two families:
- **wgrad** G1/G3/G5 — output (H,F)/(F,H), contraction **K = N (small)**. Big
  58.7 M-elem outputs, tiny K → *memory-bound on the 117 MB output write*.
- **dgrad** G2/G4/G6 — output (N,·) small, contraction **K = H or F (large)**,
  read a full 117 MB weight → *small-M GEMM, memory-bound on the weight read*.

Everything else (gather, row-scale, row-dot, two gating muls, SiLU′, add, two
scatters, ~11 fp32 casts in the reference) is cheap and **memory-bound** —
pure fusion fodder. `token_indices` is **sorted & unique** ⇒ scatters need no
atomics; `grad_hidden_states`/`grad_routing_weights` are mostly zeros (memset +
scatter N rows). `gate_output = silu(w1_output)` is an input, so SiLU′ only
needs `sigmoid(w1_output)` — `gate_output` need not be re-read.

## Shapes × dtype × tolerance

16 workloads, all `H=4096, F=14336`, bf16 I/O, **backward**. `N∈{64…2048}`,
`B∈{256…4096}`. Tolerance: **`max_atol` 0.0097→0.04 (grows with N), `max_rtol`
0.05, matched_ratio 0.99, no error cap.** rtol 5 % is *loose* and atol scales
with accumulation length — the grader explicitly budgeted for reduced-precision
math. **bf16 tensor-core GEMMs with fp32 accumulation are the intended lever;
the reference's fp32 compute is not required.**

## Roofline (per shape) — B200: 2250 TFLOP/s bf16, ~7.0–7.7 TB/s HBM, ridge 292 FLOP/B

**Fixed weight floor = 6·F·H·2 B = 704.6 MB → 0.092 ms @7.7 / 0.101 ms @7.0.**
This is unavoidable (3 weights read + 3 weight-grads written) and — crucially —
**equals the SOL for every small-N workload.** `LB = max(compute, mem@7.0)`.

| i | B | N | GFLOP | MB | AI | class | cmp ms | mem ms | LB ms | **SOL** | base | gap× |
|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|--|
| 0 | 512 | 64 | 45 | 718 | 63 | **mem** | .020 | .103 | .103 | .095 | 1.01 | 11 |
| 1 | 997 | 128 | 90 | 731 | 123 | **mem** | .040 | .104 | .104 | .098 | 1.60 | 16 |
| 2 | 512 | 256 | 180 | 745 | 242 | **mem** | .080 | .106 | .106 | .100 | 2.51 | 25 |
| 3 | 1571 | 196 | 138 | 745 | 185 | **mem** | .061 | .106 | .106 | .101 | 2.18 | 22 |
| 4 | 1024 | 128 | 90 | 731 | 123 | **mem** | .040 | .104 | .104 | .098 | 1.60 | 16 |
| 5 | 2689 | 336 | 237 | 774 | 306 | **mem*** | .105 | .111 | .111 | .131 | 3.11 | 24 |
| 6 | 4096 | 256 | 180 | 774 | 233 | **mem** | .080 | .111 | .111 | .110 | 2.53 | 23 |
| **7** | 4096 | **2048** | 1443 | 1023 | 1410 | **compute** | .641 | .146 | **.641** | .797 | 15.35 | 19 |
| 8 | 541 | 64 | 45 | 718 | 63 | **mem** | .020 | .103 | .103 | .095 | 1.01 | 11 |
| 9 | 2053 | 256 | 180 | 757 | 238 | **mem** | .080 | .108 | .108 | .104 | 2.52 | 24 |
| 10 | 1024 | 512 | 361 | 784 | 460 | **compute** | .160 | .112 | .160 | .200 | 4.36 | 22 |
| 11 | 1721 | 215 | 152 | 749 | 202 | **mem** | .067 | .107 | .107 | .102 | 2.29 | 23 |
| 12 | 2048 | 256 | 180 | 757 | 238 | **mem** | .080 | .108 | .108 | .104 | 2.52 | 24 |
| 13 | 3967 | 496 | 350 | 806 | 434 | **compute** | .155 | .115 | .155 | .193 | 4.27 | 22 |
| 14 | 256 | 64 | 45 | 716 | 63 | **mem** | .020 | .102 | .102 | .094 | 1.01 | 11 |
| 15 | 2371 | 296 | 209 | 765 | 273 | **mem*** | .093 | .109 | .109 | .116 | 2.84 | 25 |

*Crossover at **N ≈ 292** (where GEMM compute time = weight floor). N ≤ ~300 →
memory-bound (13 of 16 shapes, weight-bandwidth-limited ~0.10 ms); N ≥ ~500 →
compute-bound (shapes 7/10/13); shapes 5/15 are right at the knee.

**Reference waste:** the ~11 fp32 upcasts alone move the three weights as fp32
(read 117 MB bf16 → write 235 MB fp32, ×3 = ~1.05 GB of pure cast traffic,
*more than the entire minimum*), then run fp32 GEMMs (235 MB weight reads) and
several unfused fp32 elementwise passes over (N,F) tensors. Baseline is **10–25×
above SOL** — the prize is: bf16 tensor cores + stream each weight/weight-grad
exactly once + fuse away every cast and elementwise round-trip.

## Prior-art sweep
- **Library:** the 6 GEMMs are plain bf16 dense → **cuBLASLt wins** (Triton is
  only 62–70 % of cuBLAS on B200). No block-scaling/quant. Grouped-GEMM libs
  (cuDNN MoE, DeepGEMM) target *many experts* — this is a **single** expert
  backward, so they don't apply. cuBLASLt `beta=1` accumulate folds G4+G6 into
  one (N,H) buffer.
- **OSS kernel:** no drop-in — the token-scatter + routing-weight-grad +
  SiLU′-gating wrapper around the GEMMs is custom. Liger/quack cover norms/CE,
  not this. → custom fused prologue/epilogue around library GEMMs.
- **Abstraction:** memory-bound glue → **Triton** (near-terminal bandwidth,
  lower DSLs gain nothing per the verified stop rule); dense GEMMs → **cuBLASLt**;
  small-N launch overhead → **CUDA graph**.

## Candidates (ranked by EV)

1. **cuBLASLt bf16 GEMMs + 3 fused Triton glue kernels + CUDA graph** — *recommended.*
   6 cuBLASLt bf16 GEMMs (fp32 accum), G4+G6 accumulated via `beta=1`. Triton
   **K-pro** (gather rows → `grad_expert_output`; row-dot → `grad_sel_w` →
   scatter `grad_routing_weights`), **K-gate** (`grad_gated` → `grad_up` +
   `grad_w1o` via SiLU′, one read), **K-epi** (add gst_w1+gst_w3, memset +
   scatter `grad_hidden_states`). Wrap the whole graph in a **CUDA graph** so the
   ~9 launches don't eat the ~0.10 ms budget on the 13 small shapes.
   Expected **~8–12× vs baseline, within ~1.1–1.3× of SOL** across all shapes.
   Effort **M**, risk **low–med** (bf16 precision — loose tol makes it likely;
   cuBLAS small-M dgrad efficiency; launch overhead — killed by the graph).
2. **bf16 rewrite + `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")`** — *low-risk fallback / the number to beat.*
   Same op graph, drop all fp32 casts (bf16 matmuls, fp32 accum). Inductor fuses
   the elementwise/cast/gather chains, dispatches matmuls to cuBLAS, and CUDA-
   graphs small shapes. Expected **~5–8× vs baseline**; likely leaves ~1.3–2×
   vs Candidate 1 (looser small-M GEMM + scatter codegen, weaker cross-op
   fusion). Effort **S**, risk **low**. Ship first as the correctness/perf floor.
3. **Deeper custom Triton fusion, per-shape dispatch** — *marginal, high effort.*
   For memory-bound N≤~300, hand-fuse gating/SiLU′ as GEMM prologues and G4/G6
   with the epilogue to cut (N,F)/(N,H) round-trips and launches; keep cuBLAS
   for compute-bound N≥500 (Triton GEMM = 62–70 %). Low EV: the intermediates it
   saves are small vs the fixed 704 MB weight floor that already sets SOL, and
   CUDA graphs already remove the launch overhead. Effort **L**, risk **med**.

## Recommended: #1 (with #2 shipped first as fallback)

- **Kernels:** 3 Triton (K-pro, K-gate, K-epi) + 6 cuBLASLt GEMMs (G4+G6 fused
  by accumulate) = ~9 ops, all captured in one CUDA graph per shape.
- **Fusion boundaries:** K-pro produces bf16 `grad_expert_output` + `grad_routing_weights`;
  K-gate consumes G2 output, emits bf16 `grad_up`/`grad_w1o`; K-epi consumes
  G4/G6, emits `grad_hidden_states`. All GEMM operands handed to cuBLAS as bf16.
- **GEMM plan:** wgrad G1/G3/G5 — big MN, K=N; large output tiles (M,N≥128),
  Split-K if K=N<128 starves SMs on the small shapes. dgrad G2/G4/G6 — small
  M=N, large K; use cuBLASLt small-M / Split-K so the 117 MB weight read hits
  bandwidth. Per-shape: N≤~300 tune for bandwidth (memory-bound), N≥500 tune for
  TC utilization (compute-bound).
- **Epilogues:** bf16 output writes fused into the Triton glue / cuBLAS
  epilogue; never a standalone cast kernel.
- **Autotune later:** GEMM tile/Split-K per (N-bucket); Triton block sizes and
  whether to recompute `silu(w1_output)` vs read `gate_output`.

## Open questions for GPU validation
1. **bf16 precision** — does bf16-in/fp32-accum pass atol 0.01–0.04 / rtol 0.05
   at 0.99 ratio for all shapes (esp. dgrad K=14336 and the tight atol=0.0097
   shape 8)? Loose tol says yes; **confirm on device**, keep fp32-accum, fall
   back to fp32/TF32 on the dgrad if any shape fails.
2. **cuBLAS small-M/small-K efficiency** — do the N≤128 dgrad reads and K=N<128
   wgrad writes actually reach ~85–90 % HBM BW, or is Split-K / a custom
   bandwidth kernel needed?
3. **CUDA-graph payoff** — measure launch overhead as a fraction of the ~0.10 ms
   small-shape budget; confirm the graph closes the gap to SOL.
4. **Effective HBM BW** — SOL sits at ~7.5 TB/s effective on the weight floor;
   verify achievable BW under the grader's cold-L2 flush.

strategy: bf16 rewrite (drop all fp32-cast round-trips): 6 cuBLAS bf16/fp32-accum GEMMs via torch.matmul + inductor-fused gather/g

sources.content:
"""
Problem 009 — expert_token_scatter_with_weighted_forward (BACKWARD), B200.

SwiGLU expert-MLP backward with token scatter/gather. Consts H=4096, F=14336.
All I/O is bf16; the reference upcasts everything to fp32 internally purely for
"stability" — that is NOT required (tolerances are loose: rtol 0.05, atol
0.0097->0.04, matched_ratio 0.99, explicitly budgeted for reduced precision).

The whole waste in the reference is ~1 GB of fp32 cast traffic (it moves the
three 117 MB weights as fp32, runs fp32 GEMMs, and does several unfused fp32
elementwise passes over (N,F) tensors). The optimized recipe is:

  * bf16 tensor-core GEMMs with fp32 accumulation (cuBLAS via torch.matmul).
    Inputs are already bf16, so bf16xbf16->fp32-accum reproduces the reference
    fp32 GEMM essentially exactly (the exact product of two bf16 values fits in
    the fp32 accumulator); the only precision loss is bf16 rounding of the
    handful of intermediate tensors, which is far inside the tolerance budget.
  * stream each weight / weight-grad exactly once (no fp32 cast copies).
  * fuse every elementwise/cast/gather round-trip. torch.compile (inductor)
    fuses the gating + SiLU' + scale/gather chains into a few Triton kernels
    and dispatches the 6 GEMMs to cuBLAS -- which the kb confirms beats any
    hand-written Triton GEMM on B200 (Triton reaches only 62-70% of cuBLAS).

6 GEMMs (all 2*N*H*F FLOPs):
  wgrad G1/G3/G5 -> grad_w2/w3/w1 (out (H,F)/(F,H), contraction K=N)
  dgrad G2/G4/G6 -> grad_gated / gst_w3 / gst_w1 (out (N,.), K=H or F)

token_indices is sorted & unique -> the two scatters need no atomics; the two
scattered outputs (grad_hidden_states, grad_routing_weights) are mostly zeros
(memset + write N rows).
"""

import torch


def _compute(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    B, H = grad_output.shape
    N = token_indices.shape[0]
    F = w1_weight.shape[0]
    device = grad_output.device

    # --- Prologue: gather N rows of grad_output --------------------------------
    # gwo = grad_output[token_indices]  (exact bf16 gather).
    gwo = grad_output.index_select(0, token_indices)              # (N, H) bf16

    # grad_routing_weights = row-dot(gwo, expert_output), scattered.
    # Reduce over H in fp32 (cheap (N,H) pass) to match the reference reduction.
    grad_sel_w = (gwo.float() * expert_output.float()).sum(dim=-1)  # (N,) fp32
    grad_routing_weights = torch.zeros(B, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_routing_weights[token_indices] = grad_sel_w.to(torch.bfloat16)

    # grad_expert_output = gwo * selected_weights[:, None]  (bf16 -> GEMM operand)
    geo = gwo * selected_weights.unsqueeze(-1)                    # (N, H) bf16

    # --- w2 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G1 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w2_weight = geo^T @ gated_output   (H, F)
    grad_w2_weight = torch.matmul(geo.transpose(0, 1), gated_output)
    # G2 (dgrad, K=H): grad_gated = geo @ w2_weight            (N, F)
    grad_gated = torch.matmul(geo, w2_weight)

    # --- gating backward (element-wise, fused) --------------------------------
    # grad_up  = grad_gated * gate_output          (bf16, feeds w3 branch GEMMs)
    grad_up = grad_gated * gate_output                            # (N, F) bf16

    # grad_gate = grad_gated * up_output ; grad_w1o = grad_gate * SiLU'(w1_output)
    # Keep the w1 branch in fp32 through the activation derivative (matches the
    # reference closely) and cast only at the GEMM boundary. grad_gate itself is
    # never materialized as a separate tensor.
    gg_f = grad_gated.float()
    w1_f = w1_output.float()
    sig = torch.sigmoid(w1_f)
    silu_grad = sig * (1.0 + w1_f * (1.0 - sig))
    grad_w1o = (gg_f * up_output.float() * silu_grad).to(torch.bfloat16)  # (N, F)

    # --- w3 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G3 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w3_weight = grad_up^T @ selected_tokens   (F, H)
    grad_w3_weight = torch.matmul(grad_up.transpose(0, 1), selected_tokens)
    # G4 (dgrad, K=F): gst_w3 = grad_up @ w3_weight                   (N, H)
    gst_w3 = torch.matmul(grad_up, w3_weight)

    # --- w1 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G5 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w1_weight = grad_w1o^T @ selected_tokens  (F, H)
    grad_w1_weight = torch.matmul(grad_w1o.transpose(0, 1), selected_tokens)
    # G6 (dgrad, K=F): gst_w1 = grad_w1o @ w1_weight                  (N, H)
    gst_w1 = torch.matmul(grad_w1o, w1_weight)

    # --- Epilogue: accumulate + scatter back ----------------------------------
    grad_selected_tokens = gst_w1 + gst_w3                        # (N, H) bf16
    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros(B, H, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_hidden_states[token_indices] = grad_selected_tokens

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )


# Compile the whole graph: inductor fuses the elementwise/cast/gather/scatter
# chains into a handful of Triton kernels and routes the 6 GEMMs to cuBLAS.
# Default mode auto-specializes per repeated shape (the benchmark setting) and
# switches to a dynamic kernel only if it observes shape variation.
try:
    _compute_compiled = torch.compile(_compute)
except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - be safe if torch.compile is unavailable
    _compute_compiled = _compute

# Once the compiled path fails at runtime we stop retrying it and use eager,
# which is numerically identical and always correct.
_use_compiled = [True]


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    args = (
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
    if _use_compiled[0]:
        try:
            return _compute_compiled(*args)
        except Exception:
            # Any compile/runtime issue -> permanently fall back to eager bf16.
            _use_compiled[0] = False
    return _compute(*args)

strategy: BF16 cuBLAS-backed mm/addmm GEMMs with torch.compile reduce-overhead fusion for gather/gating/scatter, accumulating the

sources.content:
"""
Problem 009 — expert_token_scatter_with_weighted_forward (BACKWARD), B200.

SwiGLU expert-MLP backward with token scatter/gather. Consts H=4096, F=14336.
All I/O is bf16; the reference upcasts everything to fp32 internally purely for
"stability" — that is NOT required (tolerances are loose: rtol 0.05, atol
0.0097->0.04, matched_ratio 0.99, explicitly budgeted for reduced precision).

The whole waste in the reference is ~1 GB of fp32 cast traffic (it moves the
three 117 MB weights as fp32, runs fp32 GEMMs, and does several unfused fp32
elementwise passes over (N,F) tensors). The optimized recipe is:

  * bf16 tensor-core GEMMs with fp32 accumulation (cuBLAS via torch.mm/addmm).
    Inputs are already bf16, so bf16xbf16->fp32-accum reproduces the reference
    fp32 GEMM essentially exactly (the exact product of two bf16 values fits in
    the fp32 accumulator); the only precision loss is bf16 rounding of the
    handful of intermediate tensors, which is far inside the tolerance budget.
  * stream each weight / weight-grad exactly once (no fp32 cast copies).
  * fuse every elementwise/cast/gather round-trip. torch.compile (inductor)
    fuses the gating + SiLU' + scale/gather chains into a few Triton kernels
    and dispatches the 6 GEMMs to cuBLAS -- which the kb confirms beats any
    hand-written Triton GEMM on B200 (Triton reaches only 62-70% of cuBLAS).

6 GEMMs (all 2*N*H*F FLOPs):
  wgrad G1/G3/G5 -> grad_w2/w3/w1 (out (H,F)/(F,H), contraction K=N)
  dgrad G2/G4/G6 -> grad_gated / gst_w3 / gst_w1 (out (N,.), K=H or F)
G4+G6 are accumulated with addmm_ so cuBLAS can use beta=1 instead of writing
two separate (N,H) temporaries and launching a standalone add.

token_indices is sorted & unique -> the two scatters need no atomics; the two
scattered outputs (grad_hidden_states, grad_routing_weights) are mostly zeros
(memset + write N rows).
"""

import torch


def _compute(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    B, H = grad_output.shape
    N = token_indices.shape[0]
    F = w1_weight.shape[0]
    device = grad_output.device

    # --- Prologue: gather N rows of grad_output --------------------------------
    # gwo = grad_output[token_indices]  (exact bf16 gather).
    gwo = grad_output.index_select(0, token_indices)              # (N, H) bf16

    # grad_routing_weights = row-dot(gwo, expert_output), scattered.
    # Reduce over H in fp32 (cheap (N,H) pass) to match the reference reduction.
    grad_sel_w = (gwo.float() * expert_output.float()).sum(dim=-1)  # (N,) fp32
    grad_routing_weights = torch.zeros(B, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_routing_weights.index_copy_(0, token_indices, grad_sel_w.to(torch.bfloat16))

    # grad_expert_output = gwo * selected_weights[:, None]  (bf16 -> GEMM operand)
    geo = gwo * selected_weights.unsqueeze(-1)                    # (N, H) bf16

    # --- w2 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G1 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w2_weight = geo^T @ gated_output   (H, F)
    grad_w2_weight = torch.mm(geo.transpose(0, 1), gated_output)
    # G2 (dgrad, K=H): grad_gated = geo @ w2_weight            (N, F)
    grad_gated = torch.mm(geo, w2_weight)

    # --- gating backward (element-wise, fused) --------------------------------
    # grad_up  = grad_gated * gate_output          (bf16, feeds w3 branch GEMMs)
    grad_up = grad_gated * gate_output                            # (N, F) bf16

    # grad_gate = grad_gated * up_output ; grad_w1o = grad_gate * SiLU'(w1_output)
    # Keep the w1 branch in fp32 through the activation derivative (matches the
    # reference closely) and cast only at the GEMM boundary. grad_gate itself is
    # never materialized as a separate tensor.
    gg_f = grad_gated.float()
    w1_f = w1_output.float()
    sig = torch.sigmoid(w1_f)
    silu_grad = sig * (1.0 + w1_f * (1.0 - sig))
    grad_w1o = (gg_f * up_output.float() * silu_grad).to(torch.bfloat16)  # (N, F)

    # --- w3 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G3 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w3_weight = grad_up^T @ selected_tokens   (F, H)
    grad_w3_weight = torch.mm(grad_up.transpose(0, 1), selected_tokens)
    # G4 (dgrad, K=F): grad_selected_tokens = grad_up @ w3_weight     (N, H)
    grad_selected_tokens = torch.mm(grad_up, w3_weight)

    # --- w1 branch -------------------------------------------------------------
    # G5 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w1_weight = grad_w1o^T @ selected_tokens  (F, H)
    grad_w1_weight = torch.mm(grad_w1o.transpose(0, 1), selected_tokens)
    # G6 (dgrad, K=F): accumulate grad_w1o @ w1_weight into the G4 output.
    grad_selected_tokens.addmm_(grad_w1o, w1_weight)

    # --- Epilogue: accumulate + scatter back ----------------------------------
    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros(B, H, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_hidden_states.index_copy_(0, token_indices, grad_selected_tokens)

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )


# Compile the whole graph: inductor fuses the elementwise/cast/gather/scatter
# chains into a handful of Triton kernels and routes the GEMMs to cuBLAS.
# reduce-overhead enables CUDA-graph capture for the launch-heavy small-N cases.
# Keep a default compiled fallback so a capture-specific failure does not force
# the whole benchmark down to eager mode.
try:
    _compute_compiled_fast = torch.compile(_compute, mode="reduce-overhead")
except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - be safe if torch.compile is unavailable
    _compute_compiled_fast = None

try:
    _compute_compiled_default = torch.compile(_compute)
except Exception:  # pragma: no cover
    _compute_compiled_default = None

# 0: fast compile, 1: default compile, 2: eager. Once a tier fails at runtime,
# stop retrying it.
_compile_tier = [0 if _compute_compiled_fast is not None else 1]


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    args = (
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
    if _compile_tier[0] == 0:
        try:
            return _compute_compiled_fast(*args)
        except Exception:
            _compile_tier[0] = 1
    if _compile_tier[0] == 1 and _compute_compiled_default is not None:
        try:
            return _compute_compiled_default(*args)
        except Exception:
            _compile_tier[0] = 2
    return _compute(*args)

import math
from collections import OrderedDict

import torch

try:
    import triton
    import triton.language as tl

    _HAS_TRITON = True
except Exception:
    triton = None
    tl = None
    _HAS_TRITON = False


if _HAS_TRITON:

    @triton.jit
    def _prologue_kernel(
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        grad_expert_output,
        grad_routing_weights,
        H: tl.constexpr,
        BLOCK_H: tl.constexpr,
    ):
        row = tl.program_id(0)
        offs = tl.arange(0, BLOCK_H)
        src_row = tl.load(token_indices + row)

        go = tl.load(grad_output + src_row * H + offs).to(tl.float32)
        expert = tl.load(expert_output + row * H + offs).to(tl.float32)
        weight = tl.load(selected_weights + row).to(tl.float32)

        tl.store(grad_expert_output + row * H + offs, go * weight)
        grad_weight = tl.sum(go * expert, axis=0)
        tl.store(grad_routing_weights + src_row, grad_weight)

    @triton.jit
    def _gate_kernel(
        grad_gated_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        w1_output,
        grad_up,
        grad_w1_output,
        TOTAL: tl.constexpr,
        BLOCK: tl.constexpr,
    ):
        offsets = tl.program_id(0) * BLOCK + tl.arange(0, BLOCK)
        mask = offsets < TOTAL

        gg = tl.load(grad_gated_output + offsets, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)
        gate = tl.load(gate_output + offsets, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)
        up = tl.load(up_output + offsets, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)
        w1 = tl.load(w1_output + offsets, mask=mask, other=0.0).to(tl.float32)

        sig = tl.sigmoid(w1)
        silu_grad = sig * (1.0 + w1 * (1.0 - sig))

        tl.store(grad_up + offsets, gg * gate, mask=mask)
        tl.store(grad_w1_output + offsets, gg * up * silu_grad, mask=mask)

    @triton.jit
    def _hidden_epilogue_kernel(
        grad_selected_tokens,
        token_indices,
        grad_hidden_states,
        N: tl.constexpr,
        H: tl.constexpr,
        LOG_N: tl.constexpr,
        BLOCK_H: tl.constexpr,
    ):
        row = tl.program_id(0)
        block = tl.program_id(1)
        offs = block * BLOCK_H + tl.arange(0, BLOCK_H)

        lo = tl.full((), 0, tl.int32)
        hi = tl.full((), N, tl.int32)
        for _ in tl.static_range(0, LOG_N):
            mid = (lo + hi) // 2
            val = tl.load(token_indices + mid, mask=mid < N, other=2147483647)
            take_right = val < row
            lo = tl.where(take_right, mid + 1, lo)
            hi = tl.where(take_right, hi, mid)

        pos = lo
        found_val = tl.load(token_indices + pos, mask=pos < N, other=-1)
        found = found_val == row
        vals = tl.load(
            grad_selected_tokens + pos * H + offs,
            mask=found & (offs < H),
            other=0.0,
        )
        tl.store(grad_hidden_states + row * H + offs, vals, mask=offs < H)


class _Buffers:
    __slots__ = (
        "grad_expert_output",
        "grad_gated_output",
        "grad_up",
        "grad_w1_output",
        "grad_selected_tokens",
        "grad_hidden_states",
        "grad_routing_weights",
        "grad_w1_weight",
        "grad_w2_weight",
        "grad_w3_weight",
        "graph",
    )

    def __init__(self, grad_output, token_indices, w1_weight):
        batch_seq_len, hidden_dim = grad_output.shape
        num_tokens = token_indices.shape[0]
        ffn_dim = w1_weight.shape[0]
        device = grad_output.device
        dtype = grad_output.dtype

        self.grad_expert_output = torch.empty(
            (num_tokens, hidden_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_gated_output = torch.empty(
            (num_tokens, ffn_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_up = torch.empty((num_tokens, ffn_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype)
        self.grad_w1_output = torch.empty(
            (num_tokens, ffn_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_selected_tokens = torch.empty(
            (num_tokens, hidden_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )

        self.grad_hidden_states = torch.empty(
            (batch_seq_len, hidden_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_routing_weights = torch.empty(
            (batch_seq_len,), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_w1_weight = torch.empty(
            (ffn_dim, hidden_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_w2_weight = torch.empty(
            (hidden_dim, ffn_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.grad_w3_weight = torch.empty(
            (ffn_dim, hidden_dim), device=device, dtype=dtype
        )
        self.graph = None

    def outputs(self):
        return (
            self.grad_hidden_states,
            self.grad_routing_weights,
            self.grad_w1_weight,
            self.grad_w2_weight,
            self.grad_w3_weight,
        )


_GRAPH_CACHE = OrderedDict()
_GRAPH_CAPTURE_FAILED = False


def _ceil_log2(x: int) -> int:
    return max(1, (x - 1).bit_length())


def _can_use_fast_path(
    grad_output,
    token_indices,
    selected_tokens,
    w1_output,
    gate_output,
    up_output,
    gated_output,
    expert_output,
    selected_weights,
    w1_weight,
    w2_weight,
    w3_weight,
) -> bool:
    if not _HAS_TRITON:
        return False
    if not grad_output.is_cuda or grad_output.dtype is not torch.bfloat16:
        return False
    if token_indices.dtype is not torch.int64 or token_indices.shape[0] == 0:
        return False
    tensors = (
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
    return all(t.is_cuda and t.is_contiguous() for t in tensors)


def _graph_key(args):
    grad_output = args[0]
    device_index = grad_output.device.index
    if device_index is None:
        device_index = torch.cuda.current_device()
    shape_key = tuple(tuple(t.shape) for t in args)
    ptr_key = tuple(t.data_ptr() for t in args)
    return (device_index, shape_key, ptr_key)


def _execute_fast(
    grad_output,
    token_indices,
    selected_tokens,
    w1_output,
    gate_output,
    up_output,
    gated_output,
    expert_output,
    selected_weights,
    w1_weight,
    w2_weight,
    w3_weight,
    buffers,
):
    batch_seq_len, hidden_dim = grad_output.shape
    num_tokens = token_indices.shape[0]
    ffn_dim = w1_weight.shape[0]

    buffers.grad_routing_weights.zero_()
    _prologue_kernel[(num_tokens,)](
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        buffers.grad_expert_output,
        buffers.grad_routing_weights,
        H=hidden_dim,
        BLOCK_H=hidden_dim,
        num_warps=8,
    )

    torch.mm(
        buffers.grad_expert_output.t(),
        gated_output,
        out=buffers.grad_w2_weight,
    )
    torch.mm(
        buffers.grad_expert_output,
        w2_weight,
        out=buffers.grad_gated_output,
    )

    total_ffn = num_tokens * ffn_dim
    block = 1024
    _gate_kernel[(triton.cdiv(total_ffn, block),)](
        buffers.grad_gated_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        w1_output,
        buffers.grad_up,
        buffers.grad_w1_output,
        TOTAL=total_ffn,
        BLOCK=block,
        num_warps=4,
    )

    torch.mm(
        buffers.grad_up.t(),
        selected_tokens,
        out=buffers.grad_w3_weight,
    )
    torch.mm(
        buffers.grad_w1_output.t(),
        selected_tokens,
        out=buffers.grad_w1_weight,
    )

    torch.mm(
        buffers.grad_up,
        w3_weight,
        out=buffers.grad_selected_tokens,
    )
    buffers.grad_selected_tokens.addmm_(
        buffers.grad_w1_output,
        w1_weight,
        beta=1.0,
        alpha=1.0,
    )

    block_h = 1024
    _hidden_epilogue_kernel[(batch_seq_len, triton.cdiv(hidden_dim, block_h))](
        buffers.grad_selected_tokens,
        token_indices,
        buffers.grad_hidden_states,
        N=num_tokens,
        H=hidden_dim,
        LOG_N=_ceil_log2(num_tokens),
        BLOCK_H=block_h,
        num_warps=4,
    )


def _run_fast(args):
    global _GRAPH_CAPTURE_FAILED

    if _GRAPH_CAPTURE_FAILED or not hasattr(torch.cuda, "CUDAGraph"):
        buffers = _Buffers(args[0], args[1], args[10])
        _execute_fast(*args, buffers)
        return buffers.outputs()

    key = _graph_key(args)
    cached = _GRAPH_CACHE.get(key)
    if cached is not None:
        _GRAPH_CACHE.move_to_end(key)
        cached.graph.replay()
        return cached.outputs()

    buffers = _Buffers(args[0], args[1], args[10])
    try:
        _execute_fast(*args, buffers)
        torch.cuda.synchronize(args[0].device)

        graph = torch.cuda.CUDAGraph()
        with torch.cuda.graph(graph):
            _execute_fast(*args, buffers)
        buffers.graph = graph

        _GRAPH_CACHE[key] = buffers
        return buffers.outputs()
    except Exception:
        _GRAPH_CAPTURE_FAILED = True
        buffers = _Buffers(args[0], args[1], args[10])
        _execute_fast(*args, buffers)
        return buffers.outputs()


def _run_torch_fallback(
    grad_output,
    token_indices,
    selected_tokens,
    w1_output,
    gate_output,
    up_output,
    gated_output,
    expert_output,
    selected_weights,
    w1_weight,
    w2_weight,
    w3_weight,
):
    batch_seq_len, hidden_dim = grad_output.shape

    grad_weighted_output = grad_output.index_select(0, token_indices)
    grad_selected_weights = (
        grad_weighted_output.float() * expert_output.float()
    ).sum(dim=-1)
    grad_routing_weights = torch.zeros(
        (batch_seq_len,), device=grad_output.device, dtype=torch.bfloat16
    )
    grad_routing_weights[token_indices] = grad_selected_weights.to(torch.bfloat16)

    grad_expert_output = grad_weighted_output * selected_weights.unsqueeze(-1)

    grad_w2_weight = grad_expert_output.t().matmul(gated_output)
    grad_gated_output = grad_expert_output.matmul(w2_weight)

    grad_up = grad_gated_output * gate_output
    sigmoid_w1 = torch.sigmoid(w1_output.float())
    silu_grad = sigmoid_w1 * (1.0 + w1_output.float() * (1.0 - sigmoid_w1))
    grad_w1_output = (grad_gated_output.float() * up_output.float() * silu_grad).to(
        torch.bfloat16
    )

    grad_w3_weight = grad_up.t().matmul(selected_tokens)
    grad_w1_weight = grad_w1_output.t().matmul(selected_tokens)

    grad_selected_tokens = grad_up.matmul(w3_weight)
    grad_selected_tokens.addmm_(
        grad_w1_output,
        w1_weight,
        beta=1.0,
        alpha=1.0,
    )

    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros(
        (batch_seq_len, hidden_dim), device=grad_output.device, dtype=torch.bfloat16
    )
    grad_hidden_states[token_indices] = grad_selected_tokens

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    args = (
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
    if _can_use_fast_path(*args):
        return _run_fast(args)
    return _run_torch_fallback(*args)

strategy: bf16 tensor-core rewrite: 6 cuBLAS bf16/fp32-accum GEMMs (torch.matmul), G6+add folded via addmm beta=1, every intermedi

sources.content:
import torch

# Keep cuBLAS bf16 GEMMs on fp32 accumulation (the numerically-safe default).
# This is the whole point of the design: bf16 tensor-core inputs, fp32 accum.
torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = False


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    """
    SwiGLU expert-MLP backward with token scatter/gather, B200-optimized.

    Strategy (see strategy.txt / DESIGN.md): the reference's fp32 upcasts are a
    stability choice, not a spec.  The intended lever is bf16 tensor-core GEMMs
    with fp32 accumulation.  We run all 6 GEMMs in bf16 via torch.matmul
    (cuBLAS), stream each weight / weight-grad exactly once, and keep every
    intermediate bf16 so nothing is materialized in fp32.

    The single exception is the routing-weight gradient: it is a row-dot
    reduction over H=4096 that feeds a (B,)-shaped output whose non-zero
    fraction (N/B) can reach ~0.5.  bf16 products there drift enough that ~1.5%
    of the non-zero elements can exceed atol, which is too close to the 0.99
    match-ratio gate -- so that reduction alone is done in fp32, matching the
    reference bit-for-bit on those elements.
    """
    B, H = grad_output.shape
    N = token_indices.shape[0]
    F = w1_weight.shape[0]
    device = grad_output.device
    idx = token_indices

    # ---- Prologue: gather N rows (bf16), routing-weight grad (fp32), row-scale ----
    # Gather only the N needed rows of grad_output as bf16 (the reference upcasts
    # the whole (B,H) tensor to fp32 first -- pure waste we avoid).
    gwo = grad_output.index_select(0, idx)                          # (N, H) bf16

    # grad_routing_weights = rowsum(gwo * expert_output) scattered into zeros(B).
    # fp32 reduction -> matches the reference exactly on the sensitive elements.
    grad_sel_w = (gwo.float() * expert_output.float()).sum(dim=1)   # (N,) fp32
    grad_routing_weights = torch.zeros(B, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_routing_weights.index_copy_(0, idx, grad_sel_w.to(torch.bfloat16))

    # grad_expert_output = gwo * selected_weights  (bf16 operand for the GEMMs)
    geo = gwo * selected_weights.unsqueeze(1)                       # (N, H) bf16

    # ---- w2 branch ----
    # G1 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w2_weight = geoᵀ @ gated_output   -> (H, F)
    grad_w2_weight = geo.t().matmul(gated_output)                  # (H, F) bf16
    # G2 (dgrad, K=H): grad_gated = geo @ w2_weight           -> (N, F)
    grad_gated = geo.matmul(w2_weight)                             # (N, F) bf16

    # ---- element-wise gating ----
    grad_gate = grad_gated * up_output                             # (N, F) bf16
    grad_up = grad_gated * gate_output                            # (N, F) bf16

    # ---- w3 branch (up projection) ----
    # G3 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w3_weight = grad_upᵀ @ selected_tokens -> (F, H)
    grad_w3_weight = grad_up.t().matmul(selected_tokens)          # (F, H) bf16
    # G4 (dgrad, K=F): grad_selected_tokens_w3 = grad_up @ w3_weight -> (N, H)
    gst = grad_up.matmul(w3_weight)                               # (N, H) bf16

    # ---- SiLU' + w1 branch (gate projection) ----
    # gate_output = silu(w1_output); d(silu)/dx = sig*(1 + x*(1 - sig))
    sig = torch.sigmoid(w1_output)                                # (N, F) bf16
    dsilu = sig * (1 + w1_output * (1 - sig))                     # (N, F) bf16
    grad_w1o = grad_gate * dsilu                                  # (N, F) bf16
    # G5 (wgrad, K=N): grad_w1_weight = grad_w1oᵀ @ selected_tokens -> (F, H)
    grad_w1_weight = grad_w1o.t().matmul(selected_tokens)        # (F, H) bf16
    # G6 (dgrad, K=F) fused with the gst_w1 + gst_w3 add via cuBLAS beta=1:
    #   gst = gst_w3 + grad_w1o @ w1_weight
    gst = torch.addmm(gst, grad_w1o, w1_weight)                  # (N, H) bf16

    # ---- scatter accumulated token grad back into zeros(B, H) ----
    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros(B, H, dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=device)
    grad_hidden_states.index_copy_(0, idx, gst)

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )

strategy: bf16 torch.compile implementation using cuBLAS tensor-core GEMMs, fused Inductor glue, bf16 scatter outputs, and addmm_

sources.content:
import torch


try:
    torch.set_float32_matmul_precision("high")
    torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True
    torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_bf16_reduced_precision_reduction = True
except Exception:
    pass


def _impl(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    batch_seq_len = grad_output.shape[0]

    grad_weighted_output = torch.index_select(grad_output, 0, token_indices)

    grad_selected_weights = torch.sum(
        grad_weighted_output * expert_output, dim=1, dtype=torch.float32
    ).to(torch.bfloat16)
    grad_routing_weights = grad_output.new_zeros((batch_seq_len,))
    grad_routing_weights.index_copy_(0, token_indices, grad_selected_weights)

    grad_expert_output = grad_weighted_output * selected_weights[:, None]

    grad_w2_weight = grad_expert_output.transpose(0, 1).matmul(gated_output)
    grad_gated_output = grad_expert_output.matmul(w2_weight)

    sigmoid_w1 = torch.sigmoid(w1_output)
    silu_grad = sigmoid_w1 * (1.0 + w1_output * (1.0 - sigmoid_w1))
    grad_w1_output = grad_gated_output * up_output * silu_grad
    grad_up_output = grad_gated_output * gate_output

    grad_w1_weight = grad_w1_output.transpose(0, 1).matmul(selected_tokens)
    grad_w3_weight = grad_up_output.transpose(0, 1).matmul(selected_tokens)

    grad_selected_tokens = grad_up_output.matmul(w3_weight)
    grad_selected_tokens.addmm_(grad_w1_output, w1_weight)

    grad_hidden_states = torch.zeros_like(grad_output)
    grad_hidden_states.index_copy_(0, token_indices, grad_selected_tokens)

    return (
        grad_hidden_states,
        grad_routing_weights,
        grad_w1_weight,
        grad_w2_weight,
        grad_w3_weight,
    )


try:
    _compiled_impl = torch.compile(_impl, mode="reduce-overhead", dynamic=False)
except Exception:
    _compiled_impl = None


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    grad_output: torch.Tensor,
    token_indices: torch.Tensor,
    selected_tokens: torch.Tensor,
    w1_output: torch.Tensor,
    gate_output: torch.Tensor,
    up_output: torch.Tensor,
    gated_output: torch.Tensor,
    expert_output: torch.Tensor,
    selected_weights: torch.Tensor,
    w1_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w2_weight: torch.Tensor,
    w3_weight: torch.Tensor,
):
    if _compiled_impl is not None:
        try:
            return _compiled_impl(
                grad_output,
                token_indices,
                selected_tokens,
                w1_output,
                gate_output,
                up_output,
                gated_output,
                expert_output,
                selected_weights,
                w1_weight,
                w2_weight,
                w3_weight,
            )
        except Exception:
            globals()["_compiled_impl"] = None

    return _impl(
        grad_output,
        token_indices,
        selected_tokens,
        w1_output,
        gate_output,
        up_output,
        gated_output,
        expert_output,
        selected_weights,
        w1_weight,
        w2_weight,
        w3_weight,
    )
